channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: GOP's immigration pitch to New Jersey Latinos aims for some distance from Trump in governor’s race
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — At a Spanish-speaking town hall, the Republican candidate for New Jersey governor assured two young Mexican immigrants who were brought to the United States as children that people without criminal records would not face deportation.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: House committee releases new Epstein documents, including ex-Trump secretary interview
item.description: (The Hill) -- The House Oversight Committee on Friday published fresh files connected to the late Jeffrey Epstein’s criminal dealings, including the transcript of a previous interview with former Labor Secretary Alex Acosta, who served under President Trump during his first term.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: I-5 freeway to temporarily shut down for live fire exercise at Camp Pendleton
item.description: Governor Gavin Newsom has criticized the federal government for allowing a live-fire military demonstration at Camp Pendleton to take place near the heavily trafficked I-5 freeway, prompting a temporary closure of the highway and causing potential disruption to the local economy.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, KRON4

item.title: No SNAP benefits to be issued in November as government shutdown stretches on
item.description: Local governments are warning citizens that because the federal government has been shut down for weeks with no end in sight, November SNAP benefits will not be issued.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months, Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: RECALL: Walmart warns dressers could fall over
item.description: Walmart has issued a recall for a product that the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission says poses a "risk of serious injury or death.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAA allows Boeing to increase 737 Max production nearly two years after door plug flew off plane
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration said Friday it will allow Boeing to produce more 737 Max airplanes by increasing the monthly limit that it imposed after a door plug blew off an Alaska Airlines jet that the company built.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT and JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas teens arrested in killing of Marine veteran working as rideshare driver
item.description: Harris County authorities arrested three teenage boys in the killing of Marine veteran Quoc "Jake" Nguyen, who was shot while driving for Uber.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man sought to 'evade prosecution through deportation' with surrender: Prosecutors
item.description: The man was facing methamphetamine distribution charges and had indicated he'd plead guilty once he took a trip to see his grandmother.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Five cities hit with federal presence remain on edge
item.description: (NewsNation) — President Donald Trump has touted the success of his deployment of the National Guard and other federal agents to crack down on immigration and crime, but residents in these cities say this presence has damaged their sense of safety.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: News stories show that reporters may have left the Pentagon, but they haven't stopped working
item.description: Two days after dozens of journalists left their desks at the Pentagon behind rather than agree to government-imposed rules on how they report about the U.S. military, it's apparent they haven't stopped working. Reporters have relied on sources to break and add nuance to stories about U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother alleges son was sexually assaulted on Sallisaw school grounds
item.description: "I dropped my son off Tuesday morning thinking, you know, he's going to be safe," Beth said. "His whole world is just changed in a matter of 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Phelan, NewsNation

item.title: Philadelphia officials seek tips about Kada Scott, who disappeared 2 weeks ago
item.description: Authorities in Philadelphia say they are no closer to locating a missing 23-year-old woman who disappeared from her nursing home job nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DA files for motion to resentence former youth minister convicted of sexual battery
item.description: The D.A. said that because she was found guilty, Mississippi law prohibits offenders from serving their sentence on house arrest.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nguyen, NewsNation

item.title: Why charges were dropped for man who abandoned Trooper during Hurricane Milton
item.description: According to court documents, charges against Giovanny Aldama Garcia, of Ruskin, were dropped this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, NewsNation

item.title: 78-year-old man missing in Oregon may have fallen in hole; police ask for help
item.description: In a phone call with his family, Thomas Weightman said he had fallen into a hole and did not know where he was.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deployment of National Guard to Chicago
item.description: The Trump administration has asked the Supreme Court to let it deploy the National Guard to the Chicago area amid President Trump’s push to send military troops into Democratic-led cities.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Peak of Orionid meteor shower nears: How and when to watch
item.description: If the supermoon wasn't enough, October is treating skygazers to another celestial delight – the peak of the Orionid meteor shower.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Routine school day turns tragic as staffer dies after student allegedly boots her in chest: DA
item.description: Staff member Amy Morrell died after a 14-year-old student allegedly kicked her in the chest during an attempted departure from the dorm at a Massachusetts therapeutic school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From bills to berets: Notable moments from NYC's contentious mayoral debate
item.description: New York City’s mayoral candidates have duked it out in their first general election debate, with contenders Zohran Mamdani, Andrew Cuomo and Curtis Sliwa sparring over everything from the Israel-Hamas war to the city’s education system.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: LA county reaches tentative $828M agreement to settle 400 more claims of sexual abuse
item.description: Los Angeles County has reached a tentative $828 million agreement to settle more than 400 additional sexual abuse claims against county employees that were not included in an earlier settlement that was the largest sexual abuse settlement in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prince Andrew to give up royal title amid Epstein controversy
item.description: Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ty Dolla $ign returns with ‘Tycoon,’ his first solo album in five years
item.description: The inventive R&B singer has just released his fifth studio album, his first full-length solo release in five years.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tensions rise ahead of 'No Kings' protests: What to know
item.description: Tensions have risen between critics and allies of the Trump administration as the country approaches the second "No Kings Day" of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WGN 9

item.title: Daniel Cameron banking on his name ID to overcome lackluster fundraising in Kentucky Senate race
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Making his pitch to connect with a Republican crowd that instantly recognized him, Kentucky Senate candidate Daniel Cameron likened the gathering to a family reunion — a feel-good reminder of his status as a party stalwart in the Bluegrass State.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI arrests alleged pro-Hamas Oct. 7 attacker living in Louisiana
item.description: The FBI has arrested a suspect in Louisiana, identified as Mahmoud Amin Ya'qub Al-Muhtadi, who allegedly participated in Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023 attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about the devastating Alaska storm and the evacuation effort
item.description: Hundreds of residents from remote Alaska Native villages are being airlifted after a storm battered their low-lying communities over the weekend, wiping away homes and killing at least one person. Two others are missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UFO? Military operation? Questions surround balloon seen above Colorado mountains
item.description: Colorado is no stranger to strange objects floating or flying across the sky (remember "Balloon Boy" from October 2009?), and many residents wanted answers for a strange balloon seen floating through the state on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An army colonel who led a lightning-fast coup takes over as Madagascar's president
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — An army colonel who seized power in a military coup was sworn in as Madagascar’s new leader Friday in a lightning-fast power grab that ousted the president and sent him fleeing the country and into hiding. Col.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and NQOBILE NTSHANGASE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Meta adding AI chatbot safety features for teens
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Instagram and Facebook, plans to roll out new safety features for its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Homeland Security says Marine's father who was deported had faced domestic violence, assault charges
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The father of a Marine who was arrested by immigration authorities when visiting his pregnant daughter at Camp Pendleton has a criminal record that includes charges of domestic violence and aggravated assault with a deadly weapon, the Department of Homeland Security said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Susan Stamberg, first woman to host a national news program, dies at age 87
item.description: Susan Stamberg, a “founding mother” of National Public Radio and the first female broadcaster to host a national news program, has died. She was 87. Stamberg died Thursday, NPR reported. It did not provide a cause of death.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 9 people have now been arrested in connection with the mass shooting in Mississippi, FBI says
item.description: LELAND, Miss. (AP) — Nine people have now been arrested in connection with a mass shooting in the Mississippi Delta town of Leland that left six people dead, an FBI special agent said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil can freely travel around US as he fights his deportation case, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has lifted travel restrictions for Mahmoud Khalil, allowing the Palestinian activist to speak at rallies and other events across the U.S. as he fights his deportation case brought by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 9- and 10-year-old charged with attempted murder, rape of 5-year-old
item.description: The Cuyahoga County Prosecutor’s Office announced charges against two juveniles following an assault on a 5-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the colleges rejecting Trump's funding compact
item.description: Multiple colleges are rejecting a compact the Trump administration sent to nine universities at the beginning of October that guaranteed funding advantages if the institutions agreed to certain policy changes.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks the Trump administration from firing workers during the government shutdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s administration from firing workers during the government shutdown, saying the cuts appeared to be politically motivated and were being carried out without much thought. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4
